CREATE TABLE test_connections (id INT PRIMARY KEY, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_connections (id, description) VALUES (1, 'First test connection');
INSERT INTO test_connections (id, description) VALUES (2, 'Second test connection');
SELECT connection_id() AS connection_id_value, id FROM test_connections WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, (SELECT connection_id() FROM test_connections WHERE id = 2) AS second_connection_id FROM test_connections;
SELECT id FROM test_connections WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_connections WHERE id = 1);
SELECT connection_id() FROM (SELECT id FROM test_connections) AS subquery;
SELECT c.id, (SELECT connection_id() FROM test_connections WHERE id = c.id) AS connection_value FROM test_connections c;
SELECT id, connection_id() FROM test_connections ORDER BY id;
SELECT id, connection_id() AS conn_id FROM test_connections WHERE description LIKE 'First%';
SELECT COUNT(*) AS connection_count, connection_id() FROM test_connections GROUP BY id;
